LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE MAY-09-2024 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF RYAN SMITH'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE FOR NONCOMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1324. (SEE OPP. 5:8-19.) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING WITH A COVER LETTER STATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-26-2025 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-01-2024 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Mar-25-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-13-2024 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Hearing re: DEFENDANT HASHICORP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant's demurrers to causes of action one and two in the second amended complaint are sustained without leave to amend.

The court previously granted summary adjudication on causes of action one and two and the Court of Appeal affirmed that ruling. (4/1/22 Order Granting Summary Judgment; Smith v. Hashicorp, Inc. (2023) A165389 at *2.) Plaintiff therefore may not attempt to relitigate those claims and the re-minted causes of action fail as a matter of law. (See CCP section 437c(n)(1) ["If a motion for summary adjudication is granted, at the trial of the action, the cause or causes of action - as to the motion that has been granted shall be deemed to be established and the action shall proceed as to the cause or causes of action - remaining."]; Pinter-Brown v. Regents of University of California (2020) 48 Cal.App.5th 55, 99 ["When the trial court adjudicated the retaliation claim, the judgment as to that issue was final and could not be revived."]; Abadjian v. Superior Court (1985) 168 Cal.App.3d 363, 370 ["A summary adjudication is binding upon the trial court prior to judgment unless an appellate court vacates the summary adjudication on pretrial review"]; St. Paul Mercury Ins. Co. v. Frontier Pacific Ins. Co. (2003) 111 Cal.App.4th 1234, 1249 ["Ordinarily, parties may not relitigate issues summarily adjudicated."]

Court to review the proposed order submitted by Defendant HashiCorp, Inc.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: Nguyen. (301/RCE)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-29-2024 Continued To Sep-13-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HASHICORP, INC.'s DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT.

Continued to September 13, 2024, to be heard by Judge East.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-20-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. BRADLEY R. BOWLES, Esq. of Bowles & Verna LLP (Tel: 925-935-3300) for plaintiff;
Atty. JADE BUTMAN, Esq. of SV Employment Law Firm PC (Tel: 650-265-0222) for defendant Hashicorp, Inc.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF RYAN SMITH's Motion for Leave to File Second Amended Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion for leave to file second amended complaint is granted. California courts "liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89.) A proposed amended complaint's validity is not considered when deciding leave to amend. (See Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.) The trial date is not until December 9.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-09-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. MASON ARIA, Esq. of Bowles & Verna LLP (tel: 925-935-3300) for Plaintiff.
Atty. LAUREN BABST, Esq. of SV Employment Law Firm PC (tel: 650-265-0222) for Defendant Hashicorp, Inc.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF RYAN SMITH's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified.

Plaintiff's motion for leave to file second amended complaint is denied without prejudice for noncompliance with CRC 3.1324. (See Opp. 5:8-19.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter stating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
